methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Terrestrial Energy is a public-stage nuclear energy company that has raised $280M to date. The nuclear energy sector contains a narrow peer universe of 11 total comparables: 3 public entities (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private companies including Oklo, Nano Nuclear Energy, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power.

Peer-median valuation proved inapplicable to this analysis. The nuclear energy sector lacks sufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage peers meeting minimum density thresholds—only 2 same-stage comparables were identified, falling short of the required 3+ datapoint minimum for reliable median extraction. Applying an all-sector median would introduce material distortion given the heterogeneity in technology maturity, capital intensity, and regulatory pathways within nuclear energy development. No triangulated valuation range can therefore be supported by this dataset.

The primary constraint is the thinness of the nuclear energy peer universe at comparable stages and financial scales. Any valuation conclusion would require either (1) expansion of the comp set via adjacent clean-energy infrastructure or advanced manufacturing sectors, (2) reliance on precedent transactions or financing rounds among the identified private peers, or (3) explicit cash-flow or technology-milestone-based discounted cash flow modeling. Without these additional data inputs, quantitative valuation synthesis is not defensible.
